snippets.title: Peter Yang on agents and coding
snippets.takeaway: Why HTML turned out to be the foundation for agentic video making from @liu8in: “We’ve been trying to build a video agent.
snippets.summary: Peter Yang (petergyang on X) shared 3 recent updates. Why HTML turned out to be the foundation for agentic video making from @liu8in: “We’ve been trying to build a video agent. However, we learned the hard way that agents have no visual intelligence. So that’s when we turned to code. HTML is the LLM’s native language. LLMs can express not only information, but also visual aesthetics through HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.

snippets.title: Guillermo Rauch on agents and tools
snippets.takeaway: Coding agents will squeeze every ounce of IKEA effect out of you, if you let them. Happy Father's Day.
snippets.summary: Guillermo Rauch (rauchg on X) shared 3 recent updates. Coding agents will squeeze every ounce of IKEA effect out of you, if you let them. Happy Father's Day. Thankful to my dad who spent every last penny on buying our family 'the bicycle of the mind' The team cooked on performance. 'Everything the light touches' was optimized Simba. Painting, layout, WebGPU shaders, blocking scripts. Every frame scrutinized.

snippets.title: Aaron Levie on agents and coding
snippets.takeaway: Agents will use software 100X more than people. When that happens, theres a huge need for guardrails on what the agents are doing so they don’t leak data or change the wrong...
snippets.summary: Aaron Levie (levie on X) shared 2 recent updates. Agents will use software 100X more than people. When that happens, theres a huge need for guardrails on what the agents are doing so they don’t leak data or change the wrong information, authoritative sources of truth for them to work with, logging and auditing of what they’re doing, the ability to collaborate with people through these systems, and more.

snippets.title: Zara Zhang on product
snippets.takeaway: A good rule of thumb for preventing AI slop (in writing, design, etc): Is your input (the context) longer than the output?
snippets.summary: Zara Zhang (zarazhangrui on X) shared 2 recent updates. A good rule of thumb for preventing AI slop (in writing, design, etc): Is your input (the context) longer than the output? I've found that for the AI to produce quality results, my input is often 3-5 times the length of the output. If your input is much shorter than the output, it's almost certainly going to produce slop I’m talking about context, not prompt

We improved quality and grew a team. We created a non-profit whereas competiors are VC funded and have other agendas. This is our strongest week so far. I was skeptical about the multi-model routing. Seems my hinch was right.

snippets.title: Google DeepMind's Logan Kilpatrick: Why the Model Eats the Harness
snippets.takeaway: Enterprise AI is moving from chat rollout toward agentic workflows, with token budgets, rollout speed, and workflow integration becoming the practical bottlenecks.
snippets.summary: So I'm excited to talk to you about everything from AgenTik AI to AI coding, world models, more today, and right off the heels of Google IO. When I talk to companies in the enterprise, they say, Everyone's talking about agentic AI, but the only place they've seen agents really working is coding agents.
